issues.title: MethodToolCallback: Incorrect Deserialization of List<T> with Class<?>
issues.body:
**Bug description**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is about.
Within the MethodToolCallback class, in the buildMethodArguments method, specifically the following implementation:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f42a31e-9324-4dc5-b1cd-53dbd5a71e21)

The current implementation in buildTypedArgument uses Class<?> to determine the target type for deserialization. This approach can lead to deserialization failures for generic types like List<T> because the specific type parameter T is lost. The implementation here should use Jackson's JavaType instead of Class<?>. Using JavaType would allow Jackson to retain the complete generic type information, enabling the correct deserialization of generic collections like List<T>, where the specific type T can be properly identified.


**Environment**
JDK21 Spring-AI 1.0.0-M6

**Steps to reproduce**
Define tool method like this，items is actually of the type List\<LinkedHashMap\>.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42c9cd5e-123e-4c73-b3a3-520c7866d91e)

**Expected behavior**
items should be List<SourcePoItemDO>
